name: Parametric Polymorphism (Generics)
def: Code written once over a type parameter that works uniformly for all types.
problem: Reuse algorithms and containers across types without losing type safety or duplicating code.
mechanism: Type parameters (List<T>, Vec<T>) are instantiated either by monomorphisation (code per type) or type erasure / boxing (one shared implementation).
limits: Erasure → boxing costs and lost runtime type info
whenNot: When a single concrete type is all you need
myths: "Generics are free everywhere" — cost model differs sharply (monomorph vs erasure)
